name: Roast Beef in Bourbon Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the oil, bourbon, onion, garlic, pepper, mustard, salt, and vinegar in a bowl. Mix well.
2. Place the meat in a plastic bag or glass dish. Pour the marinade over the meat and marinate for 1 hour at room temperature, turning the meat 2 to 3 times.
3. Drain the meat, reserving the marinade. Spit the meat from end to end through the center or on a diagonal, ensuring that the roast is balanced.
4. If the meat has not been tied already, tie it after fastening it in place with spit forks for a firmer texture.
5. Engage the spit and turn the meat 8 to 10 inches above hot coals for 1 1/2 hours or until a meat thermometer registers 120 degrees F (rare) to 165 degrees F (well-done).
6. Brush with the sauce during the last 30 minutes of cooking.
7. For grilling, place the meat over a drip pan with hot coals surrounding it, then turn and baste as needed to cook evenly. Grill for 1 1/2 to 2 1/2 hours.
8. Let the meat stand on a platter for 20 to 30 minutes before carving. Serve the sauce with the meat.
